summary: Backend regression tests for PIN setup/verify, utility bill payments (12 categories), recharge PIN enforcement, wallet PIN checks, Firebase config, and admin login. 32/33 tests pass. Found 1 real bug in /api/auth/me that causes the Profile UI to report 'PIN not set' even after successful setup.

critical.issue: Always returns has_pin:false even after a successful /auth/setup-pin. Root cause: in get_current_user() (server.py line 83-84) `user.pop('pin_hash', None)` is called BEFORE /auth/me reads `user.get('pin_hash') is not None`. So has_pin is always False for any request that goes through get_current_user. Verified: POST /auth/setup-pin returns 200, POST /auth/verify-pin with the same PIN returns 200 (proves PIN persisted in DB), but GET /auth/me still returns has_pin:false. This is exactly the 'Profile PIN setup was not saving' symptom from the UI – the PIN does save, but the UI cannot tell.

action_items: Fix /api/auth/me has_pin calculation. Simplest fix: in /auth/me handler (server.py ~line 341-356), re-fetch the user doc from Mongo (await db.users.find_one({'_id': ObjectId(user['_id'])})) and use that doc's pin_hash for has_pin. Alternatively, attach `has_pin` to the returned user dict inside get_current_user BEFORE popping pin_hash.

No other backend fixes required. All new bill-payment endpoints (/api/bills/categories, /api/bills/billers/{cat}, /api/bills/pay, /api/bills/history), recharge PIN enforcement, wallet PIN enforcement, and Firebase public config endpoint are working as specified.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2193 lines – violates the 'split files >700 lines' guideline. Recommend splitting into modules (auth, wallet, recharge, bills, admin, mlm).

Bill payment and recharge cashback/coin credit operations do multiple separate $inc update_one calls per request – should be combined into a single update or a Mongo transaction for atomicity (partial failure could leave coins deducted but balance untouched).

POST /api/bills/pay currently requires `pin` as a required pydantic field, so missing pin returns 422 (not 401). That's acceptable but not uniform with other PIN-required endpoints – consider making pin Optional and doing explicit 401 for both missing and invalid pin to give a consistent error contract.
Random `status = random.choice([...])` produces non-deterministic bill/recharge status which is fine for simulation, but consider gating behind a dev flag so production behaviour is deterministic.

get_current_user pops pin_hash but server.py still assumes it is present in `/auth/me` – classic stale-reference bug surfaced by this test run. Recommend a small helper `async def serialize_user(u, include_pin_flag=True)` to avoid reuse errors.
test_credentials: Admin mobile=9999999999 password=Admin@123 pin=1234. Fresh users registered per-session via /api/auth/register with random 10-digit mobile.
seed_data_creation: Registered one TEST_User account per test session (random mobile) and set PIN=5678. No manual DB seeding.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_pin_bills_recharge.py. Covers admin login, firebase config (projectId=rida786), PIN setup/verify (one failing on /auth/me has_pin – see action_items), 12 bill categories and per-category billers, /bills/pay PIN + amount + balance validation, /bills/history, recharge PIN enforcement (missing pin -> 401, invalid pin -> 401, valid pin with 0 balance -> 400 insufficient), and wallet self-transfer/user-transfer/withdraw invalid PIN -> 401. After the /auth/me fix lands, re-run only the TestPinFlow class to confirm has_pin goes True after setup-pin.
rca of the issue: In /app/backend/server.py line 79-85 get_current_user loads the user from Mongo then strips sensitive fields including pin_hash before returning. /auth/me (line 341-356) then does `"has_pin": user.get("pin_hash") is not None` on that already-stripped dict, so it is always None -> has_pin is always False. Reproduction: register a new user, call POST /api/auth/setup-pin with {"pin":"5678"} (200 OK), call POST /api/auth/verify-pin with {"pin":"5678"} (200 OK confirming persistence), then GET /api/auth/me -> has_pin:false. Mitigation: re-read user doc from DB inside /auth/me OR compute has_pin inside get_current_user before the pop, e.g. `user["has_pin"] = user.get("pin_hash") is not None; user.pop("pin_hash", None)` then change /auth/me to read user["has_pin"].
